other_observers: I believe Rachel Hopper got a picture of this bird and posted it a week later. However, I am truly unsure if same bird, just believed to be.; Only birder that saw it. No one else was there
specific_location: At feeders on patio at:  Fort Collins Museum of Discovery 408 Mason Court Fort Collins, CO 80524   adjacent to Poudre River Bike Trail
description: A mostly gray and white bird with flecks of brown on sides of back and black streaks. Completely white undertail coverts and rump with no streaks. Flanks had heavier, more noticeable streaks of black than males that went up to chest. Ear patchs were colored kind of a brown with the black streaks from flanks ending just below - the cheek patches seemed to have a mix of light brown and dark brown/black, but appeared more buffy. The middle breast and underbelly were white.Behaviors: This bird came in by itself to a feeder about 35 feet from me. Several feeders had about 50 Common Redpolls at them already. The flock was skittish and after 5 minutes, it flew with the Common Redpolls all together to trees across the bike path west of the Science Center's feeders.Call: not heardPlumage: winter
